description: Scaffold the ~/alga-license repo: package.json, TypeScript config, linter, test runner, directory layout
HTTP server bootstrap (Fastify/Express) with a /healthz readiness endpoint
Config/env loading: private-key file path, port, service-auth secret, entitlement-store DB connection
Dockerfile + k8s deployment manifest for Nine Minds-hosted deploy
Port the signing logic from ee/tools/alga-license/sign.mjs into the service: sign a JWT from claims + private key
Round-trip verification in tests: tokens signed by the service verify against packages/licensing verify (v1-test keypair)
kid/key selection in the signer (v1 production key) so verifiers pick the right baked public key
Entitlement data model: subscription <-> current license token
Claim-code data model: code, subscription, expiry/TTL, consumed flag
Appliance registration + credential data model: appliance_id, hashed credential, subscription, last_checkin_at
Entitlement-store schema setup / migrations
POST /sign: accept issuance params (tier, seats, cust, sub, term) and return a signed JWT
POST /sign authenticates the caller via the alga-psa service credential
POST /sign records/refreshes the current token for the subscription in the entitlement store
POST /sign input validation: tier in {pro,premium}, valid term, numeric seats; exp computed from term (connected ~31d / air-gap 365d)
POST /claim-codes: mint a one-time claim code for a subscription (called by C3, service-auth)
POST /register: accept a one-time claim code
POST /register validates the claim code (exists, not consumed, not expired)
POST /register issues a per-appliance credential and binds the appliance to the subscription
POST /register returns { appliance_credential, first_jwt, check_in_url }
POST /register consumes the claim code (single-use, idempotent under concurrent calls)
Reinstall/rebind: minting a fresh claim code revokes the prior appliance credential
POST /check-in authenticates via the appliance credential
POST /check-in returns the current signed JWT for the appliance's subscription
POST /check-in returns a 'no change' result when the appliance already holds the current version
POST /check-in stops serving fresh tokens when the subscription entitlement is inactive (soft revocation)
POST /check-in updates last_checkin_at
Check-in payload is thin/inspectable: credential + current version in, signed JWT out, no telemetry
POST /revoke (or status update): mark a subscription's entitlement inactive so check-in stops serving
Structured error responses for invalid/expired/consumed claim code, revoked appliance, bad sign params
Define a 'license-order' service-request type/template registered in the SR provider/template system
License-order form field: tier (pro/premium)
License-order form field: seats (number, min 1)
License-order form field: term/transport (connected-monthly | connected-annual | air-gap-annual)
Portal entry point to start a License-order request
License-order form validation (required fields, seats numeric, valid term)
License-order submission stored as a service_request_submission with the order payload
Register the license-order provider in the enterprise provider registry
License-order request appears in the portal's existing service-request list/status
New dedicated Stripe products/prices config for appliance licenses (env keys; price lookup by tier x term)
Create a Stripe Checkout Session from a license-order submission
Stamp metadata into the session: service_request_submission_id, tier, seats, term, transport, client_id
Select the correct SKU/price from tier x term
Map connected term -> subscription mode, air-gap term -> annual term in Checkout
Redirect the operator to the hosted Checkout after submission
Checkout success/cancel return URLs back to the portal
Webhook: handle checkout.session.completed for license orders, distinguished from SaaS-seat/other events
Webhook idempotency via payment_webhook_events for license-order events
Webhook correlates the event back to the submission via metadata
Webhook fires the issuance workflow on a completed license-order payment
Handle Checkout session expiration/cancel: no issuance occurs
Alga workflow triggered off the completed-payment event
Workflow starts the Temporal issuance run with id license-issue:{payment_intent_id} (idempotent)
Activity: derive exp/term from the order (connected short ~31d / air-gap 365d)
Activity: call C4 /sign with issuance params and receive the JWT
Activity: record the C4-side entitlement (via /sign) keyed to the Stripe subscription
Activity: upsert a client_contracts assignment for the client (tier/term/start/end)
Activity: upsert a license contract_line capturing tier/seats
Activity: set renewal_mode on the contract for air-gap annual
Activity: store the signed JWT as a document (entity_type='client')
Activity: associate the document to the contract (entity_type='contract')
Activity (connected): call C4 /claim-codes to mint a one-time claim code
Activity: deliver the issuance email (claim code for connected / JWT for air-gap) + portal link
Idempotency: re-running the workflow for the same payment_intent_id yields one contract, one document, one code
Sign-failure handling: a failed /sign retries via Temporal without double-issuing
Renewal path: subscription renewal extends the contract end_date and re-signs (connected) / re-delivers (air-gap)
Cancellation path: subscription cancel calls C4 /revoke and marks the contract terminated
Air-gap pre-expiry renewal nudge: contract renewal workflow re-delivers a fresh annual JWT before expiry
Issuance email templates: claim-code variant and JWT variant
createUser consults resolveSelfHostTier seats in self-host mode (license_state present)
essentials tier imposes no seat limit (unmetered)
EE tiers (pro/premium) enforce the license seats at user creation
SaaS path (tenants.licensed_user_count) is unchanged when no license_state row exists
Clear error message when the appliance seat limit is reached
Migration: add appliance_id, check_in_url, appliance_credential, last_checkin_at to license_state
In-app License page: 'Connect this appliance' claim-code entry control
Server action: redeem claim code -> call C4 /register -> store credential + first JWT + check_in_url in license_state
On successful registration, set license_state.license_token to the first JWT
App-side scheduled refresh job (daily) that calls C4 /check-in
Refresh job overwrites license_state.license_token when a new JWT is returned
Refresh job no-ops on a 'no change' check-in result
Refresh job handles a revoked/stop result: leaves the current token to grace-expire
Grace/degradation: when license_token exp passes with no refresh, resolveSelfHostTier drops to essentials (verify wiring)
License page shows connected status + last check-in time
Reconnect/rebind: entering a new claim code replaces the stored credential
Air-gap path unaffected: the existing paste-a-license control still works alongside connect
Claim-code redemption error handling (invalid/expired/consumed code -> clear message)
getClientContracts() action: fetch a client's license contracts (tier/term/expiry/status)
New Licenses tab/route in the client portal at a route distinct from the account stub
Render the license contracts list (tier/term/expiry/renewal status)
Per-contract 'Download key' action linking to the stored document
Key download served via the existing portal Documents mechanism
Empty state when the client has no license contracts
Portal nav entry for the Licenses view
Access control: a portal user only sees their own client's license contracts and documents
Shared, versioned request/response contract types for C4 endpoints (sign/register/check-in/claim-codes/revoke)
alga-psa C4 client module (callers for sign/register/check-in/claim-codes/revoke) with service-credential auth
Config/env: C4 base URL + service credential in alga-psa
Documentation of the appliance-license Stripe SKUs and the C4 API contract (which price = which tier x term)
End-to-end happy path wired: portal order -> pay -> issuance -> contract+doc+code -> connect -> refresh
